text.title: Microservices Service Boundaries Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for cutting microservice boundaries correctly.

rule.statement: Each microservice MUST own exactly one DDD bounded context. Two services sharing a bounded context is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Shared context = shared language = duplicated logic + diverging models; the boundary is fictitious.

rule.statement: Each service MUST own its data. Cross-service DB joins, shared schemas, or direct table reads from another service are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Shared DB couples services to each other's schema; any migration breaks all consumers.

rule.statement: A single business transaction MUST be local to one service (or use a saga). Distributed 2PC across services is forbidden.
rule.rationale: 2PC has poor latency and availability properties; saga or local tx is the only practical pattern.

rule.statement: Every public service surface MUST have a versioned, registry-stored contract (OpenAPI for REST, protobuf for gRPC, Avro/JSON Schema for events). Inline schemas are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Boundary IS the contract — without a registered contract there is no boundary to defend.

rule.statement: Each service MUST be owned by ≤1 team. Two teams co-owning one service is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Conway's law: shared ownership produces fragmented design; clear ownership unblocks decisions.
